Title: Potato tuber expression of Arabidopsis WRINKLED1 increase triacylglycerol and membrane lipids while affecting central carbohydrate metabolism

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated oleosin transcripts are associated with oil droplet formation and likely improved TAG packaging in transgenic tubers.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Oleosin 1/2/3 transcripts increased strongly in AtWRI1 tubers. 2. Structural analysis detected oil droplets in transgenic tuber cells but not wild type. 3. Authors infer oil-body organization capacity is present but still limited in tubers.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; light microscopy/TEM showing oil droplets; lipid profiling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low in control tubers, strongly induced in AtWRI1-expressing tubers, especially at S3.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in greenhouse tubers of line 8016 during active development (S3).
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: These were not the transgene itself and are presented mainly as responsive downstream genes; included because the paper highlights them as important for TAG organization. Authors suggest stronger protection of TAG may require further engineering.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate genes for stacking with WRI1 to enhance stable oil-body formation and reduce TAG exchange with membrane lipids.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not directly tested; insufficient oleosin capacity may permit excessive exchange between TAG and membrane lipids.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and field tuber development stages; tuber-specific transgene expression via GBSS promoter.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy-dense storage lipid of nutritional and industrial value.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated PDCT is associated with altered DAG/PC interconversion and the increased 18:2 enrichment seen in TAG and DAG of transgenic tubers.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PDCT transcript increased ~5.7-fold at S3 in AtWRI1 tubers. 2. TAG and DAG became enriched in 18:2 and depleted in 18:3. 3. Authors discuss extensive interaction between TAG and membrane lipid pools, likely involving PC-related exchange.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; TAG/DAG/polar lipid compositional profiling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low-moderate in control tubers and upregulated in line 8016 across development.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in developing transgenic tubers, most evident at S3.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly functionally manipulated in this study; evidence is correlative as a downstream responsive gene.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could increase acyl exchange with membrane lipids rather than exclusive TAG deposition.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tuber development stage under greenhouse conditions.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage lipid with nutritional and industrial uses.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Tuber-specific expression of AtWRI1 markedly increases TAG and polar membrane lipids in potato tubers, while decreasing starch content and massively increasing soluble sugars.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tuber-specific AtWRI1 expression increased TAG up to ~20-fold in greenhouse tubers and up to 125-fold in a field tuber fraction, reaching about 0.8–1.1% DW in individual field tubers. 2. AtWRI1 also increased polar lipids and shifted TAG/DAG fatty acid composition toward higher 18:2 and lower 18:3. 3. Central carbohydrate metabolism was strongly altered, including reduced starch, increased glucose/sucrose/fructose, altered TCA intermediates, and changes in starch granule morphology and starch-bound lipids.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic tuber-specific overexpression under GBSS promoter; lipid profiling by TLC/GC; metabolite profiling by GC-MS; RNA-seq; microscopy/TEM; field and greenhouse phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in transgenic tubers at all developmental stages S2-S4; driven by tuber-specific GBSS promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Fatty acid biosynthesis, precursor supply through plastid glycolysis and associated carbon metabolism, and downstream oil accumulation in tubers
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or is associated with upregulation of plastid glycolysis genes, pyruvate dehydrogenase subunits, ACCase components, fatty acid synthesis genes, stearoyl-ACP desaturase, oleate desaturase, glycerol-3-phosphate dehydrogenase, PDCT, and oleosin genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Pyruvate kinase; phosphoglyceromutase; enolase; NADP-malic enzyme; pyruvate dehydrogenase subunits; heteromeric ACCase subunits; malonyl-CoA:ACP malonyltransferase; ACP3; ACP5; KASI; KASIII; ENR; KAR; HAD; SAD; FAD2/oleate desaturase; G3P dehydrogenase; PDCT; oleosins
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator sufficient to trigger ectopic oil accumulation in a starch-storing tuber organ, though not sufficient for high seed-like TAG deposition.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation/induction of lipid biosynthetic and precursor-supplying genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Transgenic expression driven by potato GBSS promoter
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is the central experimental gene of the paper. Effects extend beyond canonical WRI1 targets, likely including secondary metabolic consequences such as Rubisco shunt-related and sugar/starch alterations. Expression in non-tuber tissues is minimized because WRI1 can negatively affect photosynthetic tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering factor for inducing oil in potato tubers; likely best deployed in multigene stacks with DGAT and oil-body proteins to improve TAG sequestration and reduce membrane lipid diversion.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Smaller tubers, altered tuber morphology, reduced starch content, huge soluble sugar accumulation, altered starch granules, and line-specific growth defects (notably line 8022).
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and field growth; developmental stages S2-S4; tuber-specific expression
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Solanum tuberosum GBSS promoter, tuber-specific constitutive within tubers
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy-dense storage oil of nutritional and economic value.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A potato LEC1 homolog is strongly induced in AtWRI1 tubers and is proposed to contribute to induction of oleosin and other seed-like oil-storage functions.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LEC1 homolog transcript was upregulated >1000-fold at S3 in line 8016. 2. Its induction coincided with strong oleosin upregulation and oil droplet appearance. 3. Authors suggest LEC1 may underlie secondary seed-like regulatory effects beyond direct WRI1 action.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression correlated with oleosin induction and oil-droplet phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Nearly absent in control tubers, highly induced in AtWRI1 tubers, strongest at S3.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly upregulated in developing transgenic greenhouse tubers.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Oil-body formation/storage deposition associated with seed-like lipid accumulation program
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Likely promotes oleosin-encoding genes; inferred from known LEC1 function and observed concomitant oleosin induction
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent secondary regulator that may help explain why oil-body related genes become induced in AtWRI1 tubers.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Induced downstream of AtWRI1 expression or associated metabolic state
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly manipulated; evidence is indirect/correlative. Included because the Results section specifically highlights its dramatic induction and potential relevance to oil accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage oil with nutritional and industrial value.
